rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use implementation annexes for bindings and assignments that are valid in that scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections in the input AADL model
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are inserted in scopes where the referenced identifiers are visible
Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees in scopes where the referenced ports are not visible
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are provided
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees in scopes where the referenced ports are not visible
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
